_meta.description: Random quotes from Robert E. Howard's Conan stories, films, and the Hyborian universe for display on character sheets

quotes.text: To crush your enemies, see them driven before you, and to hear the lamentation of their women.

Crom, I have never prayed to you before. I have no tongue for it. No one, not even you, will remember if we were good men or bad. Why we fought, or why we died. All that matters is that two stood against many. That's what's important! Valor pleases you, Crom... so grant me one request. Grant me revenge! And if you do not listen, then to hell with you!
Only pain.
He is Conan, Cimmerian. He won't cry, so I cry for him.
Look at the strength in your body, the desire in your heart, I gave you this!
Enough talk!
What good is a sword against sorcery?
I'll find my kingdom, and a queen to sit beside me.
I live, I love, I slay, and I am content.
When a Cimmerian feels thirst, it is a thirst for blood.
No man shall live in chains.
No man may have me, unless he's beaten me in a fair fight.
If danger is a trade, I'll learn it by myself.
I know my future. You have none.
You have nothing more to learn, Sonja. You are the master of the master. Never have I seen your equal. You must learn to like men a little better.
If you yield only to a conqueror, then prepare to be conquered.
The winds of the steppes carry no mercy for the weak—only arrows and dust.
A queen's crown is forged in blood, not gold; I'll wear mine atop a mountain of foes.
By the serpent's coils, I'll unravel any spell that dares bind me.
Frost and fury are my kin; no southern heat can melt a Vanir's resolve.
The forest whispers secrets to those who listen—and death to those who trespass.
Elephants may trample the earth, but a clever blade fells even the mightiest beast.
Sail with me, and the seas will yield treasures beyond your wildest greed.
quotes.source: Belit, Queen of the Black Coast

quotes.text: Armor is for fools who fear the bite of steel; I wear only my scars with pride.
Ride hard, strike true, and let the gods sort the fallen.
The black lotus blooms in dreams, but awakens only nightmares for the unwary.
Cimmerian hills breed iron men; soft lands make only shadows.
Gold flows like rivers in Ophir, but I'll dam it with my enemies' bones.
Set's priests hide in shadows, but my light is the flash of a drawn dagger.
From the frozen wastes, I come with Ymir's breath to chill your coward's heart.
Thieves' guilds are webs of lies; I cut through them like silk.
In Vendhya's jungles, gods demand sacrifice—I'll offer them your head.
Pictish drums beat war's rhythm; dance to it or die.
quotes.source: Bran Mak Morn, Last King of the Picts

quotes.text: Hyrkanian bows sing death's song across endless plains.
Ancient Valusia's ruins hold curses that time cannot bury.
Reavers' feasts are sweetened by the cries of the conquered.
Sorcerers bargain with demons; I bargain with none but my blade.
Aquilonia's legions march in vain against the barbarian tide.
Black coasts swallow the foolish, spitting back only legends.
Crom's mountain watches silent, granting strength to the worthy.
Shemite sands hide assassins' blades, sharp as envy.
Nordheim's blizzards forge warriors from ice and rage.
Kothian forges hammer lies into steel.
Vanir raids leave only flames and vengeance in their wake.
Asgardian halls echo with sagas of gods slain.
Hyborian wars redraw maps in rivers of blood.
Thieves slip through shadows, claiming crowns from kings.
Ymir's giants crush the bold, but fear the cunning.
Stygian pyramids guard pharaohs' wrath eternal.
Pictland's spears defend the wild heart of freedom.
Conan's path is strewn with thrones trodden underfoot.
Arenas crown victors in blood-soaked glory.
Sorcerers' curses poison the air like foul mist.
Hyborian nomads roam free, unbound by stone walls.
Cimmerian pride runs deeper than any wound.
Sunken Lemuria whispers of horrors awakening.
Picts ambush in mists, silent as ghosts.
Zingaran corsairs raid under moonlit sails.
Hyrkanian shamans ride winds of prophecy.
Valusian serpents strike from forgotten tombs.
Set's hunger feeds on souls offered in darkness.
Cimmerian forges birth blades of northern fire.
Reavers' oaths bind tighter than chains.
Heroes' deeds echo through time's bloody scrolls.
Black lotus snares the mind in velvet chains.
Aquilonian rebels rise with swords of justice.
Mitra's light banishes shadows, but night returns.
Shemite bazaars trade in whispers and daggers.
Zamora's towers house spiders of intrigue.
Nordheim's snows bury the unworthy.
Kothian mercenaries sell loyalty like cheap wine.
Black kingdoms pulse with ancient rhythms.
Vanir axes cleave fates in red fury.
Asgardian gods reward the valiant dead.
Hyborian chronicles foretell the barbarian's rise.
Thieves' cunning unlocks forbidden vaults.
Vendhyan temples demand rivers of blood.
Ymir's breath freezes cowards in their tracks.
Stygian sands entomb secrets of the damned.
Pictish war-cries shatter civilized dreams.
Conan's laugh defies gods and kings.
Gladiators' blood fuels the crowd's savage joy.
Sorcerers summon abyssal terrors for power.
Hyborian caravans brave perils for spice and gold.
Cimmerian omens read doom in the stars.
Atlantean ruins curse the greedy explorer.
Picts paint victory in enemies' gore.
Zingaran wine drowns sorrows in red haze.
Hyrkanian hordes thunder like storms unleashed.
Valusian kings rule from thrones of bone.
Set's priests weave doom in serpent tongues.
Cimmerian winters temper souls for war.
Reavers' sails hunt horizons for plunder.
Heroes tread paths lined with fallen foes.
Black lotus visions lure to madness' edge.
Aquilonian banners fly high in defiance.
Mitra's faithful clash with darkness' spawn.
Shemite nomads ride with scimitars drawn.
Zamora's thieves claim shadows as kin.
Nordheim's eternal ice guards ancient secrets.

Know, oh prince, that between the years when the oceans drank Atlantis and the gleaming cities, and the years of the rise of the Sons of Aryas, there was an Age undreamed of, when shining kingdoms lay spread across the world like blue mantles beneath the stars.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: Hither came Conan, the Cimmerian, black-haired, sullen-eyed, sword in hand, a thief, a reaver, a slayer, with gigantic melancholies and gigantic mirth, to tread the jeweled thrones of the Earth under his sandalled feet.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: Civilized men are more discourteous than savages because they know they can be impolite without having their skulls split, as a general thing.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: Barbarism is the natural state of mankind. Civilization is unnatural. It is a whim of circumstance. And barbarism must always ultimately triumph.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: In this world men struggle and suffer vainly, finding pleasure only in the bright madness of battle.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: I have known many gods. He who denies them is as blind as he who trusts them too deeply.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: They have no hope here or hereafter. Their gods are Crom and his dark race, who rule over a sunless place of everlasting mist, which is the world of the dead.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: What do I know of cultured ways, the gilt, the craft and the lie? I, who was born in a naked land and bred in the open sky. The subtle tongue, the sophist guile, they fail when the broadswords sing; Rush in and die, dogs—I was a man before I was a king.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: When I was a fighting-man, the kettle-drums they beat, The people scattered gold-dust before my horses feet; But now I am a great king, the people hound my track With poison in my wine-cup, and daggers at my back.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: The more I see of what you call civilization, the more highly I think of what you call savagery!
In the old free days all I wanted was a sharp sword and a straight path to my enemies. Now no paths are straight and my sword is useless.
For man's only weapon is courage that flinches not from the gates of Hell itself, and against such not even the legions of Hell can stand.
Cimmeria—a land of darkness and deep night, where no sun ever shone, and where the people were dark and brooding like the land itself.

But in the north, the tribes are growing. These people are called Hyborians, or Hybori; their god was Bori—some great chief, whom legend made even more ancient as the king who led them into the north, in the days of the great Cataclysm.
The tale of the next thousand years is the tale of the rise of the Hyborians, whose warlike tribes dominate the western world.
A tribe of that race has discovered the use of stone in building, and the first Hyborian kingdom has come into being—the rude and barbaric kingdom of Hyperborea.
The Hyborians have encountered the Picts, driving them into the barren lands of the west.
He seemed more a part of the sun and high places of the outlands. His slightest movement spoke of steel-spring muscles knit to a keen brain.
Conan, grim, blood-stained, naked but for a loin-cloth, shackles on his mighty limbs, his blue eyes blazing beneath the tangled black mane.
I know this: if life is an illusion, then I am no less an illusion, and being thus, the illusion is real to me.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: Someday, when all your civilization and science are likewise swept away, your kind will pray for a man with a sword.
Where does barbarism leave off and civilization begin?

Yet it may be in some far day the shadows shall fade and the Prince of Darkness be chained forever in his hell. And till then mankind can but stand up stoutly to the monsters in his own heart and without.
History fair drips with blood.

Let me live deep while I live; let me know the rich juices of red meat and stinging wine on my palate, the hot embrace of white arms, the mad exultation of battle when the blue blades flame and crimson, and I am content.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: When I cannot stand alone, it will be time to die. But I would like to fall with teeth locked in a foeman's throat.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: I live, I burn with life, I love, I slay, and am content.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: He did not wonder at the strangeness of it all, not even why he should be in such a place of dread and mystery. He had ceased to wonder at the strange places his feet led him.
There comes a time in every man's life when he must face the reality of his own mortality.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: He had not learned to fear. All the world was his oyster, and he was the stone knife that would pry it open.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: In that dead citadel of forgotten ages might sleep shapes of living horror.
Men say I am a thief, but this charge I deny: I am a liberator of doomed treasure.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: Free my sword arm, and I'll make corpses of you all!
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "A Witch Shall Be Born"

quotes.text: A man's greatest gift is a sharp blade and a strong arm to wield it.
The clangor of the swords had died away, the shouting of the slaughter was hushed; silence lay on the red-stained snow.
Their path was the sword-path and red it ran through the world.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: He moved with the assurance of a panther in the dark.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Rogues in the House"

quotes.text: This is what I was born for—this and battle! Let me be a mercenary, with sword and courage for sale.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: Crom and his dark race never answered save with the clang of steel.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Phoenix on the Sword"

quotes.text: I seek not beyond death. It may be the blackness averred by the Nemedian skeptics, or Crom's realm of ice and cloud, or the snowy plains and vaulted halls of the Nordheimer's Valhalla.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: Know, oh prince, that between the years when the oceans drank Atlantis and the gleaming cities, and the years of the rise of the Sons of Aryas, there was an Age undreamed of.
What use to call on Crom? He cares not if men live or die. Better to be silent than to call his attention to you; he will send you dooms, not fortune!
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: Conan sensed that the aura of ancient evil which hung over this place was not lifted by the coming of morning.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Devil in Iron"

quotes.text: His sword was part of him, and his arm was tireless.
There was movement in the darkness and a figure emerged, tall and powerful as a leopard.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Rogues in the House"

quotes.text: He had learned that civilized men were more dangerous than savages—not physically, but in the crafty cunning of their plotting.
The wind moaned through the black branches, and there was no other sound in all the forest.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: Life was a continual battle, with no quarter asked or given.
The sands of the desert are red with the blood of kings and armies.
He was bred in the game of war, and his sword was as natural to him as his hands.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: Steel cannot harm me, but I am not ready for death.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: In that dead empire men walked the streets bearing the ghosts of their former glory.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: The night was full of sounds: the howl of distant wolves, the hiss of the wind, and the clank of armor.
Fear? I know not fear. I have looked into the abyss and the abyss blinked first.
Gold and women, they are the roots of all the trouble in the world.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Rogues in the House"

quotes.text: He fought, not because fighting was his trade, but because he loved battle.
Shadows swam in the darkness and unseen presences lurked in the black corners.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: There is always another dawn.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: Let teachers and priests and philosophers brood over questions of reality and illusion. I know this: if life is illusion, then I am no less an illusion, and being thus, the illusion is real to me.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Queen of the Black Coast"

quotes.text: He was a man against whom the world had turned, but who had not turned against the world.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: The jewels winked at him in the darkness like demon eyes.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: Death had been Conan's companion for so long that he had come to look upon the dark specter as an old comrade.
Out of the darkness came the sound of swords singing their deadly song.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "A Witch Shall Be Born"

quotes.text: The moon hung like a silver scimitar in the black velvet sky.
All fled from him who had never fled from any man.
He came into the world with teeth and claws and fists, and with teeth and claws and fists he would leave it.
Midnight found him still on his feet, his sword still dripping.
He was as one born out of his time—too early for civilization's chains, too late for the age of heroes.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: I am not a priest. I do not know why things happen as they do. I only know they happen.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Devil in Iron"

quotes.text: In this world every man must stand alone.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: There comes a time when a man must choose between the path of the warrior and the path of the wise.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: The ancient temple stood like a fist thrust up from the earth, defying the sky.
He came of a race of hillmen, accustomed to scaling forbidding crags, and he was a man of unusual strength and agility.
There was no mistaking the invitation in her eyes, nor the challenge.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "A Witch Shall Be Born"

quotes.text: In one of these dens merriment thundered to the low smoke-stained roof, where rascals gathered in every stage of rags and tatters.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: Men said he spent much time in the hills, in curious conclaves with surviving remnants of an old race.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: Aye. And when the day of reckoning came, the sword was not spared. So Acheron ceased to be.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: I am of human origin, but I rule demons. You have seen the Lords of the Black Circle.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: Once all living things worshipped him. That was long ago, when beasts and men spoke one language.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: As he talked he was leading the way deeper and deeper into the ancient wilderness.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: Shaking the sweat and blood from his eyes, Conan saw four horsemen sitting their steeds in the twilight and staring up at him.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "A Witch Shall Be Born"

quotes.text: Man of the raven locks, tell me your name, so that my brothers in Vanaheim may know who was the last of Wulfhere's band to fall before the sword of Heimdul.
Once, limned against a purple sunless horizon, he saw the mighty walls and towers of a great city such as rose nowhere on the waking earth he knew.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: The night was moonless and black, as befitted such bloody work.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "A Witch Shall Be Born"

quotes.text: Trust not to sorcery, for the cost is always more than the gain.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: He struck with such fury that his blade sang as it cut the air.
Through midnight shadows he ran, silent as a panther.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Rogues in the House"

quotes.text: Every man's hand was against him, and his hand was against every man.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Tower of the Elephant"

quotes.text: Gold has a way of vanishing, but cold steel remains.
There was a tiger's strength in his thews and a wolf's stealth in his tread.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The Hour of the Dragon"

quotes.text: The dice were loaded against him but still he played, for that was his nature.
He trusted nothing but his own senses and his sword.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "Beyond the Black River"

quotes.text: Somewhere a jackal howled and was answered by another, and another, until all the night was alive with their hideous concert.
They were the sons of barbarians, and the seed of civilization had not yet been planted in their hearts.
quotes.source: Robert E. Howard, "The People of the Black Circle"

quotes.text: His word was as good as his sword arm—and no man questioned that.
